mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contracting below its historical trend indicates supply chain disruptions or demand collapse, compressing equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO drops below 95 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings expectations, lifting S&P 500 forward returns within one week of the data release.
failure_condition: Forward S&P 500 returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth surprises over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings exceeding 3 percent trigger central bank tightening expectations, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive despite sustained CPI readings above 3 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly drops in retail sales indicate immediate consumer demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during recessionary periods.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances during mild economic downturns.
